title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a camping trip.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you checked the campsite for next weekend? || Man: Yes. It has a shop and showers, but no kitchen area, so we'll need to cook outside. || Woman: I can bring my small stove. || Man: Good. [8]The website says we must arrive before six, because the gate is locked after that[/8]. If traffic is bad, we should leave earlier than planned. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you checked the campsite for next weekend?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes. It has a shop and showers, but no kitchen area, so we'll need to cook outside.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I can bring my small stove.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Good. The website says we must arrive before six, because the gate is locked after that. If traffic is bad, we should leave earlier than planned. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: What do they need to remember?

options.A: to arrive before the gate closes
options.C: to buy food at the campsite

questions.explanation: The man says they must arrive before six because the gate is locked after that.

extracts.context: You hear a woman talking about a new pair of glasses.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: I collected my new glasses yesterday. The assistant said the red frames looked fashionable, but they were too bright for me. The black ones were comfortable, although they were almost the same as my old pair. || Speaker: [9]I chose the brown frames in the end because they suited my face and felt light[/9]. They cost a bit more, but I wear glasses every day. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: I collected my new glasses yesterday. The assistant said the red frames looked fashionable, but they were too bright for me. The black ones were comfortable, although they were almost the same as my old pair.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: I chose the brown frames in the end because they suited my face and felt light. They cost a bit more, but I wear glasses every day. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

questions.question: Which glasses did the woman choose?
questions.explanation: She says she chose the brown frames because they suited her face and felt light.

extracts.context: You hear two students talking about an art homework task.
extracts.audio_script: Boy: I thought the art homework was to paint a landscape, so I started one last night. || Girl: I nearly did that too, but I checked the school website. || Boy: Was I wrong? || Girl: A bit. [10]We have to draw an object from home using only pencil, and bring the object to class as well[/10]. The landscape task is for next week. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I thought the art homework was to paint a landscape, so I started one last night.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: I nearly did that too, but I checked the school website.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Was I wrong?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: A bit. We have to draw an object from home using only pencil, and bring the object to class as well. The landscape task is for next week. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: What is the art homework?

options.B: copy a picture from the website

questions.explanation: The girl says they have to draw an object from home using only pencil and bring it to class.

extracts.context: You hear two people talking about a hotel.
extracts.audio_script: Man: How was the hotel by the coast? The photos looked amazing. || Woman: The view was lovely, and the room was clean. Breakfast was simple, but that was fine. || Man: So you'd recommend it? || Woman: Not to anyone without a car. [11]It said the beach was five minutes away, but that was by car, not on foot[/11]. Walking there took nearly half an hour. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: How was the hotel by the coast? The photos looked amazing.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The view was lovely, and the room was clean. Breakfast was simple, but that was fine.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So you'd recommend it?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not to anyone without a car. It said the beach was five minutes away, but that was by car, not on foot. Walking there took nearly half an hour. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: What was disappointing about the hotel?

options.B: the beach was not as close as advertised

questions.explanation: The hotel advertised the beach as five minutes away, but that was by car, and walking took much longer.

extracts.context: You hear a man talking to a friend about a book club.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are you enjoying the book club at the community centre? || Man: Yes, though the first book was difficult. I liked hearing what other people thought, even when I disagreed. || Woman: Will you keep going? || Man: Definitely. [12]Next month we're choosing a shorter book, so more people should finish it before the meeting[/12]. That will make the discussion better. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you enjoying the book club at the community centre?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, though the first book was difficult. I liked hearing what other people thought, even when I disagreed.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Will you keep going?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Definitely. Next month we're choosing a shorter book, so more people should finish it before the meeting. That will make the discussion better. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: What change will the book club make next month?
questions.explanation: The man says they are choosing a shorter book so more people will finish it.

extracts.context: You hear an announcement in a sports shop.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: Good morning, shoppers. Today there is twenty percent off running shoes on the first floor. Football shirts are still full price until next weekend. || Speaker: [13]Customers who are here to collect repaired bicycles should go to the back door, where a member of staff will check your ticket[/13]. Please do not bring bicycles through the main shop area because it is very busy. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Good morning, shoppers. Today there is twenty percent off running shoes on the first floor. Football shirts are still full price until next weekend.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Customers who are here to collect repaired bicycles should go to the back door, where a member of staff will check your ticket. Please do not bring bicycles through the main shop area because it is very busy. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

questions.question: Where should customers collect repaired bicycles?
questions.explanation: The announcement says customers collecting repaired bicycles should go to the back door.
